FenceData.title: FenceData
FenceData.description: Represents the real-time state of the electric fence in Firebase Realtime Database.

status.description: The current status of the fence (e.g., 'LEGAL', 'ILLEGAL_NO_PULSE').

source.description: Indicates if the status was set by the 'web' admin panel.

Sensors.title: Sensors
Sensors.description: Represents sensor data from the IoT device in Realtime Database.

gas.description: The gas level reading from the MQ sensor.

SensorData.title: SensorData
SensorData.description: Represents sensor data from the IoT device. This is for the original dashboard and is separate from the new Realtime Database structure.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SensorData entity.

gasValue.description: The gas level reading from the MQ sensor (e.g., in PPM).

lastRead.description: Timestamp of when sensor data was last read.

definition.description: Stores the fence state (status and source) in Realtime Database.
Stores sensor data (e.g., gas) from the ESP8266 in Realtime Database.
Stores sensor data for the original dashboard. This path is in Firestore.

params.name: sensorDataId
params.description: Unique identifier for the sensor data document.

firestore.reasoning: The application uses Firebase Realtime Database for two-way data synchronization between the web app and an ESP8266 device. Data is split into two main nodes: `/fence` for device status and control source, and `/sensors` for data from the device's sensors (like gas). This flat structure is simple for a microcontroller to consume. The original Firestore structure for `sensor_data` is kept for the unmodified `/dashboard` page.
